title: Senior Director of Software Engineering
description:
Senior Director of Software Engineering — JPMorganChase, Wealth Management Technology, Jersey City NJ.

Join one of the most consequential technology transformations in financial services. At JPMorganChase, you'll have the opportunity to lead a groundbreaking AI engineering program, shaping how the firm modernizes its most critical processing systems.

Job Summary: As a Senior Director of Software Engineering within Wealth Management Technology, you will define and deliver the AI toolchain that accelerates mainframe modernization across Credit, Money Market & Mutual Funds, Statements & Tax, and IBOR domains. You will embed directly with domain subject-matter experts and build agentic systems that ingest legacy code, job schedules, data flows, and production knowledge to produce structured specifications and accelerate migration delivery. This is a hands-on program leadership role: you own the roadmap, manage cross-team governance, and write code alongside your team.

Job responsibilities:
- Design, build, and operate the end-to-end AI modernization pipeline, including artifact ingestion, specification generation, agent-driven code translation, and automated verification
- Own the strategic sequencing of migration across domains, establishing beachheads, proving the model, and scaling against business commitments and platform readiness
- Build and lead a high-performing AI engineering team, hiring, mentoring, and retaining AI engineers while setting engineering standards
- Partner directly with mainframe subject-matter experts to validate specifications and ensure migrated logic preserves business intent
- Design multi-agent systems for discovery, specification, code generation, and verification
- Collaborate with architecture teams to define the target-state platform
- Coordinate with technology leads, architecture teams, vendor partners, and senior leadership, presenting progress, trade-offs, and investment decisions to both technical and executive audiences
- Establish LLMOps practices for the AI toolchain, including deployment, monitoring, cost management, drift detection, and continuous improvement

Required qualifications:
- 10+ years applied software engineering experience; 5+ years leading technologists
- Deep hands-on experience building production LLM-based applications, agentic architectures, RAG pipelines, and LLMOps for 24/7 systems
- Distributed systems experience in 24/7 environments, event-driven and batch architectures, API design, and modern cloud platforms (AWS/EKS/ECS/Kafka)
- Demonstrated ability to build and scale engineering teams, including hiring, developing, and retaining senior technical talent
- Experience designing and implementing multi-agent orchestration systems
- Proven track record of delivering large-scale technology programs with measurable business outcomes

Preferred: Experience in financial services, particularly wealth management, brokerage, or capital markets processing. Background in program management for large-scale technology transformations.

Compensation: $232,750 - $325,000 base plus discretionary incentive and equity awards.

title: Generative AI - Group Manager - Senior Vice President
description:
Generative AI Group Manager - Senior Vice President — Citi Services Tech AI Team, Jersey City NJ.

We are seeking a results-driven Generative AI group manager with end-to-end experience for execution and deployment of cutting-edge Generative AI solutions. In this role, you will be responsible for translating AI strategy into tangible, production-ready capabilities that enhance operational efficiencies and drive business value.

Key Responsibilities:
- GenAI Delivery Leadership: Execute the delivery roadmap for generative AI projects, ensuring alignment with business objectives and timelines. Manage the project lifecycle from ideation and scoping to deployment and post-launch support.
- Team Leadership & Mentorship: Build, mentor, and manage a high-performing team of AI engineers and specialists. Foster a culture of execution, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
- End-to-End Solution Delivery: Oversee the design, development, and deployment of robust, scalable, and production-ready GenAI models.
- Stakeholder & Program Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for GenAI delivery. Manage stakeholder expectations, communicate project progress, identify and mitigate risks.
- Cross-Functional Partnership: Collaborate closely with Data Mesh, Cloud Architecture, MLOps, and business unit teams.
- Technical Excellence & Best Practices: Drive the adoption of best practices in software development (CI/CD), MLOps, and project management (Agile/Scrum).
- Governance & Ethical Deployment: Implement and enforce robust governance and ethical AI frameworks.

Required Technical Skills: LLMs & Fine-Tuning (LoRA, QLoRA, RLHF), Model Optimization (AWQ, GPTQ, vLLM), RAG techniques, MLOps, TensorFlow/PyTorch, AWS AI/ML services, NLP, containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), LangGraph, Autogen, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face.

Required qualifications: 15+ years experience; 8+ years in AI/ML with at least 3 years in Generative AI; 5+ years leadership experience managing technical teams and delivering complex software or AI solutions.

Compensation: $176,720 - $265,080 base (plus discretionary incentive and retention awards). Citi SVP roles carry significant bonus components not reflected in base salary.

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
Chief Technology Officer — fast-growing Financial Technology organization (via TeamAce Limited), New York.

Job Summary: As the Chief Technology Officer, you will be responsible for leading the company's technology strategy, scaling engineering operations, driving platform security and reliability, and building high-performing engineering teams to support rapid business growth.

Responsibilities:
- Lead and scale the engineering organization, including talent acquisition, performance management, and team development
- Define and execute the company's long-term technology strategy, architecture, security, and scalability roadmap
- Drive the development of secure, reliable, and high-performance payment and financial technology systems
- Collaborate with executive leadership to align technology initiatives with business objectives and growth plans
- Maintain hands-on involvement in engineering execution, system design, and technical decision-making

Requirements:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
- Minimum of 6 years experience in software engineering, with significant leadership experience in fintech, consumer technology, or high-growth startups
- Strong expertise in system architecture, cybersecurity, scalability, payments infrastructure, and platform reliability
- Proven track record of building and leading high-performing engineering teams

Salary: Open to negotiation. Apply: https://tinyurl.com/CHIEFTECHNOLOGYOFFICEROD
